content.full_text:
Re
Fern Polrrrcel PRACTrces C oMMrssroN
;128 J Street o Suire 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
March 5,2009
Larry Menth
Assistant Deputy Director
Labor and Human Resources
CD Forestry and Fire Protection
P.O. Box 944246
Sacramento, CA 94244-2460
Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-09-025
Dear Mr. Menth:
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 Because your-question is general in
nature, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.z
This letter is based on the facts presented; the Fair Political Practices Commission
(the "Commission") does not act as the finder of fact when it renders advice. (ln re
Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.) Also, please note that our advice is based solely on the
provisions of the Act. We therefore offer no opinion on the application, if any, of other
conflict-of-interest laws such as common law conflict of interest or Government.Code
Section 1090. Also, we offer no opinion on the application, if any, of Public Contract
Code Section 10410, Government Code Section 19990, or your agency's statement of
incompatible activities. We suggest that you consult with your agency's counsel on these
issues.
QUESTION
Does having your own consulting firm that would contract with local government
agencies, as well as private companies, to perform tasks that are similar to those you
perform for your state agency present problems with your current job as a CEA II for
CAL FIRE?
I The PoliticalReform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are kr the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections I 8l l0 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations. unless otherwise indicated.
I Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice. (Section 831 l4; Regulation 18329(cXl), copy enclosed.)


File No. l-09-025
Page No. 2
CONCLUSION
As stated above, we recommend that you consult with your agency's counsel to
discuss the provisions above; below we have given you an overview of tfr" areas of the
Act that could apply, depending on your future actions.
FACTS
You are a CEA II with the Califomia Department of Forestry and Fire protection,
CAL FIRE. As a CEA II with CAL FIRE, you deal with all matters of labor relations,
human resources, contract negotiations, classification and pay, policy and procedures,
and workers' compensation, inter alia.
You intend to establish your own consulting firm that would focus on similar
areas. The firm would contract with local governmental entities, including but not
limited to fire departments. In some circumstances, these local fire depart"ments may
have contractual relationships with CAL FIRE to provide all aspects oi fi.. protection
and the associated human resources/labor relationi services.
You may also contract with a private company to provide similar services. This
company currently contracts with CAL FIRE to provide aerial fire fighting services,
including aircraft operation and maintenance. You currently do not i'ave i professional
relationship with this private company through your employment at cAL rinr.
conflict of Interest 
ANALYSIS
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions ensure that public officials ..perform
their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their own financial interests
or the financial interests of persons who have supported them." (Section gl00l(b).)
Specifically, Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence u!ou".n-"ntut
decision in which the official has a financial interest.
You have a potential source-of-income financial interest from the local
governments and the private company for which you might consult. Income includes
monies received from any source that operates in and does business in your agency's
jurisdiction. (Section S2030(a).) Excepted from the definition of income, hoivever, is
salary and reimbursements received from a state, local, or federal government agency.
(Section 82030(bx2).) This exception applies only if you, as a consultant, file a
statement of economic interests for that agency. If you do not, the exception does not
apply, and the income you receive from the agency could disqualify you fro-
participating in govemmental decisions for your state agencyit ut would have a financial
effect on your non-state source of income.
If you are consulting with a private company, that company, as a source of
income' would be an economic interest to you. If you have any decision in your capacity
as a CEA II that involves or affects the private company, you would apply the eighi steps
outlined in the Act, as explained in the "can I vote?" brochure (copy inciosed; to
determine whether you can participate in the decision.


File No. I-09-025
Page No. 3
As a public official, if you are making govemmental decisions that have a
material financial effect on any of your economic interests, you might have a conflict of
interest that would preclude you from participating in the governmental decision. While
theAct does not prohibit you from taking on otheiactivitiis for compensation, it does
limit you as to the types of actions you can perform in your capacity as a public official.
Influence over your agency on behalfofanother
Section 87104 prohibits a state official from attempting to influence certain
actions of his or her state agency on behalf of an outsid" pl.roin.,
"No public official of a state agency shall, for compensation, act as
_an agent or attorney for, or otherwise represent, any other person
by making any formal or informal appearance before, or any oral
or written communication to, his or her state agency or any officer
or employee thereof, if the appearance or communication is for the
purpose of influencing a decision on a contract, grant, loan,
license, permit, or other entitlement for use."
(Section 87104(a).)
Thus, if you are acting on behalf of either the private company you mentioned or
a Iocal govemment agency, you would be prohibited irom utternptirg to influence the
decisions of your state agency. The prohibition in Section Bll}4uppti", when a state
employee receives compensation to influence the actions of his o. t "r agency. The
prohibition applies to formal and informal appearances and any oral or writtin
communication to CAL FIRE, if the appearance or communicition is made to influence
the agency's action on a contract, grant, loan, license, permit, or other entitlement for use.
(See Rice Advice Letter, A-08-183.)
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel
By: Heather M. Rowan
Counsel, Legal Division
(&Y4",t'l lsr'/a,'--
HMRjgl
Enclosures
3 "Person" is broadly defined in Section 82047 toinclude any individual, proprietorship, firm,
partnership, joint venture, syndicate, business trust, company, corporition, associaiion, committee, and any
other organization or group ofpersons acting in concert.

Act that could apply, depending on your future actions.
